Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1821 upvotes, 232 comments
  2. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 324 upvotes, 39 comments
  3. Mesh over Bluetooth LE, TCP, or Reticulum 102 upvotes, 11 comments
  4. Julia Snail – An Emacs Development Environment for Julia Like Clojure's Cider 84 upvotes, 10 comments
  5. Standardizing source maps 40 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. TADA: Fast, Reliable Speech Generation Through Text-Acoustic Synchronization 52 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. AutoKernel: Autoresearch for GPU Kernels 33 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 196 upvotes, 60 comments
  9. Universal vaccine against respiratory infections and allergens 268 upvotes, 87 comments
  10. Pike: To Exit or Not to Exit 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  11. Writing my own text editor, and daily-driving it 121 upvotes, 37 comments
  12. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 261 upvotes, 99 comments
  13. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 325 upvotes, 127 comments
  14. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 58 upvotes, 17 comments
  15. SSH Secret Menu 223 upvotes, 88 comments
  16. Levels of Agentic Engineering 203 upvotes, 92 comments
  17. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 43 upvotes, 15 comments
  18. Create value for others and don’t worry about the returns 386 upvotes, 226 comments
  19. Google to Provide Pentagon with AI Agents 37 upvotes, 15 comments
  20. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 217 upvotes, 130 comments
  21. Meta acquires Moltbook 507 upvotes, 345 comments
  22. When the chain becomes the product: Seven years inside a token-funded venture 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Docs directories are doomed 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 339 upvotes, 257 comments
  25. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 484 upvotes, 394 comments
  26. As US missiles leave South Korea, the Philippines asks: are we next? 14 upvotes, 6 comments
  27. RISC-V Is Sloooow 254 upvotes, 258 comments
  28. Agents that run while I sleep 340 upvotes, 385 comments
  29. I'm going to build my own OpenClaw, with blackjack and bun 40 upvotes, 34 comments
  30. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 152 upvotes, 167 comments

Last generated 7:30pm, 11 March 2026. Source here.
